channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: How to watch Algeria vs Austria: Free Streams & TV Channels for FIFA World Cup 2026 in a play-off to be Group J runners-up
item.description: Here's how to watch Algeria vs Austria for free online and from anywhere as both teams chase a place in the World Cup 2026 knockout stage. Or do they?

item.title: How to watch Jordan vs Argentina: Free Streams & TV Channels for FIFA World Cup 2026 as Lionel Messi seeks to add to his haul
item.description: Here's how to watch Jordan vs Argentina for free online and from anywhere as Lionel Messi and Co. wrap up their Group J campaign at World Cup 2026.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Sunday, June 28 (game #1616)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Sunday, June 28 (game #1113)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Sunday, June 28 (game #847)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: Quote of the day by PayPal co-founder Peter Thiel: 'I no longer believe that freedom and democracy are compatible' — a foundational thesis of the new tech elite
item.description: Technology has marched forward over the last couple of decades at the same time as basic freedoms and rights have been eroded

item.title: How to watch Jordan vs. Argentina online for free
item.description: How to watch World Cup for free. Live stream Jordan vs. Argentina in the 2026 FIFA World Cup for free from anywhere in the world.

item.title: Instagram is testing more ways to customize ‘Your Algorithm’
item.description: Instagram users could soon see more ways to tune their content.

item.title: Teenage Engineering adds lo-fi mode, USB audio, and more to its KO II sampler
item.description: Teenage Engineering has already issued multiple substantial updates for its surprisingly capable $329 EP-133 KO II sampler. Its latest is one of the biggest yet. OS 2.5 adds audio over USB, selectable sample rates for lo-fi fun, sample reverse, an arpeggiator, equal-length autochopping, and it extends the maximum length of a sample from 20 seconds […]...
